author: jtraglia
content: Hey <@&1460290804898861117> is there anything you'd like to discuss at the upcoming [ACDT#82](https://github.com/ethereum/pm/issues/2103) call? Feel free to post a message here or leave a comment on the github issue?

author: jochembrouwer
content:
Hi all, since we include EIP-7997 in the next Glam devnet I feel like these points: https://ethereum-magicians.org/t/eip-7997-deterministic-factory-predeploy/24998/26?u=jochem-brouwer are not yet fixed in the current EIP spec. CC <@411317441688371202> 

The main point is that if we insert the factory in the chain at the fork block this should be included in the BAL. If it is not included then any client which implements a quick state root check by batch writing the BAL to state, they will not insert the factory address and thus yield invalid state root. 
An alternative could be that this change MUST NOT be in the BAL and that this insertion in implicit, but I nevertheless feel this should be cleared up.
If no client currently does the "quick state root check" via BAL then this problem could go unnoticed
There are no EEST tests yet but there is this issue: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/issues/1988

I'll also agenda this for ACDT but we can likely resolve this async? 🙂

author: jtraglia
content: Thanks! Will add this to the agenda.

author: jochembrouwer
content:
Extra Q: has someone checked at what numbers we cannot [deploy](https://etherscan.io/address/0x4e59b44847b379578588920ca78fbf26c0b4956c) the [Arachnid's determinitic deployment proxy](https://github.com/Arachnid/deterministic-deployment-proxy/tree/master) anymore? (it would OOG)

Similar to all system address deployments. Would be nice to see when this breaks.

content: i think most system contracts deploy with 250k gas limt. 7002 used like 185k of that

author: lightclient
content: looks like arachnid's gas limit is 100k 😅

author: jochembrouwer
content:
Ok that will OOG anyways with EIP-8037
Deployed bytes: 69
CPSB: 1530
69 * 1530 = 105570 > 100k 😬

author: jochembrouwer
content: 504 bytes for 7002 so that also breaks (771120 code deposit extra cost)

author: jochembrouwer
content: It is not a problem but this does mean that these system contracts + Arachnid's contract must be deployed before Glamsterdam

author: jochembrouwer
content: (for system contracts this would be a problem if a chain wants to bundle forks, not sure if this ever happens, do both X + Glamsterdam)

author: jochembrouwer
content: Accounted for the gas price, not for gas limit 😢
